metadata.title: [REVIEW] Missing pagination state management pattern in hook architecture

output.summary: Task execution completed with exit code 0
output.implementationNotes:
Task execution completed. Check logs for details.

Final output:
**Findings**
- High: Notes list endpoints were discarding backend pagination metadata, so `useNote` inferred `hasMore/total` from page size alone and could show “Load More” when `has_more` was false (e.g., exact page-size last page). Fixed by normalizing note list responses in `src/api/notes.js:34` and applying server pagination in `src/hooks/useNote.js:153`.
- Medium: Legacy callers expect array-only note responses; returning `{ notes, pagination }` would have broken task/project note loaders. Kept wrappers returning arrays in `src/api/tasks.js:391` and `src/api/projects.js:399`.

Pagination state for notes now uses backend `limit/offset/total/has_more` when available, while still falling back to computed values for legacy responses. This preserves existing UI behavior and fixes incorrect “Load More” visibility.

Tests: `npm run build` (warns about missing exports in `src/services/proposalService.js` from `src/api/proposals.js`, pre-existing).

Next steps if you want extra coverage:
1) `npm test`
2) Manual check: load a project/task notes list with exactly `limit` notes and confirm “Load More” only appears when backend `has_more` is true.
